rationale.R1.2: A section review centrally requires detecting internal contradictions in the study description and argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The review should assess whether the conclusions about blind deployment and trust calibration follow from the reported surveys, hovers, and logged turns.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review normally includes attention to sentence-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty where the terse delegation does not specify a specialized review mode.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer responsibility includes flagging claims that exceed what the described protocol and measurements can support.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to review the section, so responsibilities must remain bounded to the provided artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because claims about trust evaluation methods could influence future system design, downstream misuse or overgeneralization is a relevant cross-cutting concern.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected review responsibility should be traceable to specific statements in the provided section.
